gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن أو موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (4/342)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن أو موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (4/342)
text: قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) إِنِّي جَعَلْتُ عَلَى نَفْسِي شُكْراً لِلَّهِ رَكْعَتَيْنِ أُصَلِّيهِمَا فِي الْحَضَرِ وَ السَّفَرِ أَ فَأُصَلِّيهِمَا فِي السَّفَرِ بِالنَّهَارِ فَقَالَ نَعَمْ ثُمَّ قَالَ إِنِّي أَكْرَهُ الْإِيجَابَ أَنْ يُوجِبَ الرَّجُلُ عَلَى نَفْسِهِ قُلْتُ إِنِّي لَمْ أَجْعَلْهُمَا لِلَّهِ عَلَيَّ إِنَّمَا جَعَلْتُ ذَلِكَ عَلَى نَفْسِي أُصَلِّيهِمَا شُكْراً لِلَّهِ وَ لَمْ أُوجِبْهُمَا عَلَى نَفْسِي أَ فَأَدَعُهُمَا إِذَا شِئْتُ قَالَ نَعَمْ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Safwan, from Is’haq Bin Ammar who said,

‘I said to Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup>, ‘I have made it upon myself, a gratefulness for the Sake of Allah<sup>azwj</sup>, two Cycles that I shall be Praying during the staying and the travelling. Shall I Pray these two in the travel by the day?’ So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Yes’. Then he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘My<sup>asws</sup> father<sup>asws</sup> disliked the obligation if the man obligates upon himself’. I said, ‘I did not make these two (obligatory) upon myself for the Sake of Allah<sup>azwj</sup>. That is upon me to Pray these two as a gratitude for Allah<sup>azwj</sup>, and I did not make these two an obligation upon myself. So, can I fulfill these whenever I so desire to?’ He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Yes’.
en.sarwar: 5. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from Safwan from Ishaq ibn ‘Ammar who has narrated the following: “I once said to abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, ‘I have made it upon myself to perform two Rak‘at Salat (prayer) as thanksgiving to Allah at home or on a journey’ if on a journey I can perform them during the day. He (the Imam) said, ‘Yes, you can do so.’ He (the Imam) then said, ‘I dislike a man’s making it obligatory upon himself.’ I then said, ‘I have not made them for Allah but I have made it upon myself to perform the two Rak‘at to thank Allah and I have not made it obligatory on myself if I can stop performing them when I wanted. He (the Imam) said, ‘Yes, you can do so.’”
